methods.name: ANCOVA
methods.description: Analysis of Covariance - ANOVA with continuous covariate adjustment

formula.notes: Covariates adjust the group comparison. Adding covariate:fixed_effect interaction tests homogeneity of regression slopes. Multiple covariates and factors are supported.

outputSpecifications.name: type3_tests
outputSpecifications.description: Type III tests for each model term (covariate, group, interactions)

outputSpecifications.name: parameter_estimates
outputSpecifications.description: Solution for fixed effects (intercept, covariate slopes, group effects)

outputSpecifications.name: ls_means
outputSpecifications.description: Least-squares means per group level, adjusted for covariates

outputSpecifications.name: contrasts
outputSpecifications.description: Pairwise LS mean differences between group levels

assumptions: Homogeneity of regression slopes (if no covariate:fixed_effect interaction)
Linear relationship between covariate and response

inputRoles.name: covariate
inputRoles.description: Continuous covariate(s) for adjustment (e.g., baseline value, age)

methods.name: ANOVA
methods.description: Analysis of Variance - compares means across multiple groups

formula.notes: One or more factors; interactions can be added with ':' or '*' operator

outputSpecifications.name: type3_tests
outputSpecifications.description: ANOVA table: one row per model term

methods.name: Chi-squared Test

codings.display: Pearson's Chi square test of independence between categorical variables

methods.description: Tests association between two categorical variables using Pearson's chi-squared statistic

formula.notes: Both response and fixed_effect are categorical. For RxC tables (more than 2x2).

methods.name: Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel Test

codings.display: Cochran-Mantel-Haenzel test for repeated tests of independence

methods.description: Stratified analysis for 2x2xK tables controlling for strata. Tests the null hypothesis of no association between response and group after adjusting for strata.

formula.notes: The '|' denotes stratification. Multiple strata variables produce cross-classified strata.

outputSpecifications.name: homogeneity_test
outputSpecifications.description: Breslow-Day test for homogeneity of odds ratios across strata

assumptions: Homogeneous odds ratios across strata (for common OR interpretation)

methods.name: Coefficient of Variation
methods.description: Computes the coefficient of variation (SD/mean × 100) of a numeric variable

methods.name: Count
methods.description: Counts non-missing observations

outputSpecifications.name: computed_value
outputSpecifications.description: The count of non-missing observations per group

methods.name: Cox Proportional Hazards
methods.description: Semi-parametric regression for survival data modeling the hazard function

formula.notes: Multiple covariates, factors, and interactions supported. The strata() function creates a stratified baseline hazard (one per stratum level) without estimating a coefficient for that variable.

configurations.name: ties
configurations.description: Method for handling tied event times

outputSpecifications.name: global_tests
outputSpecifications.description: Overall model significance tests (likelihood ratio, Wald, score)

outputSpecifications.name: type3_tests
outputSpecifications.description: Type III Wald tests per model term

outputSpecifications.name: parameter_estimates
outputSpecifications.description: Regression coefficients on log-hazard scale with hazard ratios

outputSpecifications.name: hazard_ratio_estimates
outputSpecifications.description: Hazard ratios with confidence intervals (exponentiated coefficients)

inputRoles.name: fixed_effect
inputRoles.description: Treatment group and/or other categorical factors

inputRoles.name: strata
inputRoles.description: Stratification variable(s) for stratified Cox model

methods.name: Cumulative Count
methods.description: Computes cumulative counts across ordered categories

outputSpecifications.name: computed_value
outputSpecifications.description: The cumulative count and percentage per category per group

methods.name: Cumulative Frequency
methods.description: Computes cumulative frequency and percentage across ordered categories

outputSpecifications.name: computed_value
outputSpecifications.description: The cumulative frequency and percentage per category per group

methods.name: Fisher's Exact Test
methods.stato_r_implementation: fisher.test(x) function, where x is a matrix
methods.description: Exact test of independence for 2x2 contingency tables. Preferred over chi-squared when expected cell counts are small.

formula.notes: Both variables must be binary for the standard 2x2 Fisher test.

methods.name: Frequency
methods.description: Computes frequency counts per category of a categorical variable

outputSpecifications.name: computed_value
outputSpecifications.description: The frequency count per category per group

methods.name: Geometric Coefficient of Variation
methods.description: Computes the geometric coefficient of variation

methods.name: Geometric Mean
methods.description: Computes the geometric mean (exp of mean of log values) of a numeric variable

methods.name: Harmonic Mean
methods.description: Computes the harmonic mean (reciprocal of mean of reciprocals) of a numeric variable

methods.name: Interquartile Range
methods.description: Computes the interquartile range (Q3 − Q1) of a numeric variable

methods.name: Kaplan-Meier Estimator
methods.description: Non-parametric survival function estimation with optional grouping

formula.notes: If no group is specified, estimates a single survival curve. With a group variable, produces stratified curves.

outputSpecifications.name: event_summary
outputSpecifications.description: Summary of events and censoring per group

outputSpecifications.name: survival_table
outputSpecifications.description: Life table with survival estimates at each event time

outputSpecifications.name: median_survival
outputSpecifications.description: Median survival time per group with confidence interval

outputSpecifications.name: landmark_estimates
outputSpecifications.description: Survival probability at specific landmark times

inputRoles.name: fixed_effect
inputRoles.description: Optional grouping variable for stratified curves

methods.name: Kurtosis
methods.description: Computes the kurtosis (measure of tailedness) of the distribution of a numeric variable

methods.name: Logistic Regression

codings.display: binomial logistic regression for analysis of dichotomous dependent variable

methods.description: Models binary outcome as function of predictors via a link function (logit by default)

formula.notes: Generalized linear model with binomial family. Multiple covariates and factors supported. For stratified analyses, can include strata as a fixed effect.

outputSpecifications.name: type3_tests
outputSpecifications.description: Type III likelihood ratio or Wald tests per term

outputSpecifications.name: parameter_estimates
outputSpecifications.description: Maximum likelihood estimates on log-odds scale

outputSpecifications.name: odds_ratio_estimates
outputSpecifications.description: Odds ratios with confidence intervals (exponentiated coefficients)

assumptions: Linear relationship between log-odds and predictors

inputRoles.name: fixed_effect
inputRoles.description: Treatment group and/or other categorical factors

methods.name: Log-rank Test
methods.description: Compares survival distributions between groups using a rank-based test

formula.notes: Multiple group factors can be included. Strata variables (after adjusting) produce stratified log-rank tests.

methods.name: Multivariate Analysis of Variance
methods.description: Tests the hypothesis of no overall treatment effect across multiple correlated response variables simultaneously

formula.notes: Left-hand side uses cbind() to specify the multivariate response. In long format with a parameter identifier, the model is fit as response ~ group with subject and parameter defining the multivariate structure.

outputSpecifications.name: type3_tests
outputSpecifications.description: Univariate Type III tests per response variable (follow-up to multivariate test)

methods.name: Maximum
methods.description: Determines the maximum observed value of a numeric variable

methods.name: Arithmetic Mean
methods.description: Computes the arithmetic mean of a numeric variable

methods.name: Median
methods.description: Computes the median (50th percentile) of a numeric variable

methods.name: Minimum
methods.description: Determines the minimum observed value of a numeric variable

methods.name: Mixed Model for Repeated Measures
methods.description: Linear mixed model for longitudinal data with structured R-side covariance. No random effects (G-side); within-subject correlation modeled through the residual covariance structure.

formula.defaultExpression: response ~ covariate + repeated_factor + fixed_effect + repeated_factor:fixed_effect | repeated_subject / repeated_factor
formula.notes: The part after '|' specifies the repeated measures structure: subject is the clustering unit, time is the repeated factor within subject. Multiple covariates and fixed effects are supported. The time:group interaction is typically the term of primary interest for treatment comparison at each visit.

outputSpecifications.name: fit_statistics
outputSpecifications.description: Model fit measures for covariance structure selection

outputSpecifications.name: type3_tests
outputSpecifications.description: Type III F-tests for each fixed effect term

outputSpecifications.name: parameter_estimates
outputSpecifications.description: Solution for all fixed effect coefficients

outputSpecifications.name: ls_means_by_group_time
outputSpecifications.description: LS means for each group at each timepoint

outputSpecifications.name: ls_means_by_group
outputSpecifications.description: Marginal LS means averaged over time

outputSpecifications.name: ls_means_by_time
outputSpecifications.description: Marginal LS means averaged over group

outputSpecifications.name: group_contrast_at_time
outputSpecifications.description: Group difference at a specific timepoint (e.g., Treatment vs Placebo at Week 24)

outputSpecifications.name: time_contrast_within_group
outputSpecifications.description: Change over time within a group (e.g., Week 24 vs Week 4 within Treatment)

outputSpecifications.name: overall_group_contrast
outputSpecifications.description: Overall group difference marginalized over time

inputRoles.name: response
inputRoles.description: The response variable at each timepoint (e.g., change from baseline)

inputRoles.name: covariate
inputRoles.description: Continuous covariate(s) (e.g., baseline value, age, weight)

inputRoles.name: repeated_factor
inputRoles.description: Timepoint/visit variable defining the repeated dimension

inputRoles.name: fixed_effect
inputRoles.description: Treatment group (and optionally additional factors like region, sex)

inputRoles.name: repeated_subject
inputRoles.description: Subject identifier for the repeated measures clustering

methods.name: Mode
methods.description: Determines the most frequently occurring value

methods.name: One-sample t-test
methods.stato_r_implementation:
t.test(x = NULL,
       alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),
       mu = 0, paired = FALSE, var.equal = FALSE,
       conf.level = 0.95, ...)


http://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-patched/library/stats/html/t.test.html
methods.description: Tests whether the mean of a single sample differs from a hypothesized value

formula.notes: Intercept-only model; the test is against the hypothesized mu

assumptions: Normality of data (or large sample size)

methods.name: Paired t-test
methods.stato_r_implementation:
http://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-patched/library/stats/html/t.test.html

t.test(dependent variable ~ independant variable, data = dataset, var.equal = FALSE, paired= TRUE)
methods.description: Tests whether the mean difference between paired observations differs from zero

formula.notes: Intercept-only model on the within-subject differences

outputSpecifications.name: point_estimate
outputSpecifications.description: Mean of paired differences with inference

inputRoles.name: response
inputRoles.description: The paired difference values (or the raw values at two timepoints)

methods.name: Percentile
methods.description: Computes the value at a specified percentile rank

configurations.name: percentile_method
configurations.description: Interpolation method (type_2 = SAS default, type_7 = R default)

outputSpecifications.name: computed_value
outputSpecifications.description: The computed percentile value per group

methods.name: Proportion
methods.description: Computes the proportion and percentage per category of a categorical variable

outputSpecifications.name: computed_value
outputSpecifications.description: The proportion, percentage, and confidence interval per category per group

methods.name: Quartile
methods.description: Computes the first quartile (Q1) and third quartile (Q3)

outputSpecifications.name: computed_value
outputSpecifications.description: The computed quartile values per group

methods.name: Range
methods.description: Computes the range (max − min) of a numeric variable

methods.name: Skewness
methods.description: Computes the skewness (measure of asymmetry) of the distribution of a numeric variable

methods.name: Standard Deviation
methods.description: Computes the standard deviation of a numeric variable

methods.name: Sum
methods.description: Computes the sum of all values of a numeric variable

methods.name: Trimmed Mean
methods.description: Computes the trimmed mean after removing a fraction of extreme values from each tail

configurations.name: trim_fraction
configurations.description: Fraction to trim from each tail (0 to 0.5)

methods.name: Two-sample t-test
methods.stato_r_implementation:
t.test(x, y = NULL,
       alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),
       mu = 0, paired = FALSE, var.equal = FALSE,
       conf.level = 0.95, ...)

http://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-patched/library/stats/html/t.test.html
methods.description: Tests whether means of two independent samples differ significantly

formula.notes: Single factor with exactly two levels

methods.name: Variance
methods.description: Computes the variance of a numeric variable

methods.name: Winsorized Mean
methods.description: Computes the Winsorized mean after replacing extreme values with boundary values

configurations.name: winsor_fraction
configurations.description: Fraction to Winsorize from each tail (0 to 0.5)

outputClassTemplates.name: type3_tests
outputClassTemplates.description: Type III (or other SS type) omnibus tests, one per model term

outputClassTemplates.name: parameter_estimates
outputClassTemplates.description: Solution for fixed effects, one row per model coefficient

outputClassTemplates.name: ls_means
outputClassTemplates.description: Least-squares means (adjusted marginal means) per factor level or level combination

outputClassTemplates.name: contrasts
outputClassTemplates.description: Pairwise or custom contrasts between LS means

outputClassTemplates.name: test_result
outputClassTemplates.description: Single overall test result (for non-regression methods)

outputClassTemplates.name: association_measures
outputClassTemplates.description: Effect size / association measures with inference

outputClassTemplates.name: homogeneity_test
outputClassTemplates.description: Breslow-Day test for homogeneity of odds ratios across strata

outputClassTemplates.name: computed_value
outputClassTemplates.description: Single derived/computed output value (for derivation methods)

outputClassTemplates.name: global_tests
outputClassTemplates.description: Overall model significance tests (likelihood ratio, Wald, score)

outputClassTemplates.name: hazard_ratio_estimates
outputClassTemplates.description: Hazard ratios with confidence intervals (exponentiated coefficients)

outputClassTemplates.name: event_summary
outputClassTemplates.description: Summary counts of events and censoring per group

outputClassTemplates.name: survival_table
outputClassTemplates.description: Life table with survival estimates at each event time

outputClassTemplates.name: landmark_estimates
outputClassTemplates.description: Survival probability at specific landmark times

outputClassTemplates.name: odds_ratio_estimates
outputClassTemplates.description: Odds ratios with confidence intervals (exponentiated coefficients)

outputClassTemplates.name: multivariate_tests
outputClassTemplates.description: Multivariate test statistics for overall hypothesis

statistics.name: geometric_mean

statistics.name: harmonic_mean
statistics.description: Harmonic mean (reciprocal of mean of reciprocals)

statistics.name: trimmed_mean
statistics.description: Trimmed mean (mean after removing extreme values from each tail)

statistics.name: winsorized_mean
statistics.description: Winsorized mean (mean after replacing extreme values with boundary values)

statistics.name: percentile
statistics.description: Percentile value at a specified rank

statistics.description: Skewness (measure of asymmetry of the distribution)
